title: The 'do...while' Loop 🛡️
description: The loop that guarantees it will run at least once before checking.

slides.title: A Loop with a Twist 🌪️
Act First, Ask Questions Later 🔫
Try It Out 💻
slides.content: Even with a false condition right out the gate, a do...while loop will execute that inner block one time.

quizData.question: What is the primary difference between a `while` loop and a `do...while` loop?

options.text: `do...while` guarantees the code block runs AT LEAST once.

quizData.explanation: Because `do...while` places its conditional check at the BOTTOM of the code block instead of the top, it will always execute exactly one time before checking if it should repeat.

slides.title: When is this useful? 🤷
slides.content:
## Real World Case

The classic use-case for a `do...while` loop is **User Prompts**.

You want to ask the user to guess a secret number. You HAVE to show them the prompt at least once. If they guess wrong, you show them the prompt again.

1. **DO**: Show the prompt and collect the guess.
2. **WHILE**: (guess !== secret_number)

This is much cleaner than creating an artificial variable just to get a standard `while` loop started!
